turns.userText: give me an overview of Mes attendance, class spread, etc. note trends and do analysis on his activity in the guild
turns.agentText:
Now I have everything I need. Let me build a chart comparing the two periods.

**Mes.1906 is one of the guild's most reliable and versatile players — 64 runs logged (84% all-time, climbing to 90% this month) with a decisive mid-season role shift from Untamed DPS to Troubadour support that is clearly visible in the numbers.**

---

## Attendance

{{figure}}

{{figure}}

Across 76 logged runs (Mar 22 – Jun 18), Mes attended **64** — putting them consistently in the **top-6 to top-8 most active squad members** in both halves of the season. Attendance has actually *improved* over time:

| Period | Runs Logged | Mes Attended | Rate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mar 22 – Apr 30 | 45 | 36 | **80%** |
| May 1 – Jun 18 | 31 | 28 | **90%** |

Last seen **June 18** — current and active.

---

## The Big Story: Role Shift (Untamed → Troubadour)

The most important thing in Mes's data is a **fundamental role change** mid-season. The attendance rollup labels them **Untamed** for the early period and **Troubadour** for the recent one. That shift ripples through every metric:

| Metric | Mar–Apr (23.1h) | May–Jun (23.6h) | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| Primary spec | Untamed | Troubadour | DPS → Support |
| Cleanses | 18,665 (~808/hr) | 32,528 (~1,378/hr) | **+70% per hour** |
| Boon Strips | 14,301 (~619/hr) | 6,612 (~280/hr) | **−55% per hour** |
| Healing | 43.2M | 53.2M | +23% |
| Deaths | 158 (6.8/hr) | 139 (5.9/hr) | −13% per hour |
| DPS | 1,631 | 1,674 | +3% |

The cleanse explosion and strip drop aren't underperformance — they're the *expected signature* of swapping from a strip-heavy melee DPS (Untamed's [[skill:Winds of Disenchantment]]-style kit) to a support-pillar spec. Troubadour is there to sustain the subgroup, and Mes's output reflects exactly that. Fewer deaths per hour is a nice bonus — possibly improved positioning, possibly survivability on the new build.

---

## Versatility: 16 Specs Played

This is the most striking flex stat in the logs. Across all 64 runs, Mes has played:

> **Troubadour, Druid, Specter, Luminary, Paragon** (support)
> **Tempest, Reaper, Scourge, Berserker, Amalgam, Firebrand, Dragonhunter, Harbinger, Untamed** (damage/utility)
> **Scrapper, Necromancer** (misc)

That's **16 distinct elite/base specs** — far above the squad average. Mes can plug into almost any slot you need filled.

---

## Summary & Takeaways

- 🟢 **Attendance is elite and trending up.** At 90% recently, Mes is a core squad pillar — show up every night, on time.
- 🔄 **The Troubadour move is working.** Cleanses per hour nearly doubled, deaths are down, healing is up. This is a successful role transition, not an experiment.
- ⚠️ **Strip output dropped significantly.** That's expected with the Troubadour, but worth knowing if you're counting on squad-wide strip coverage — Mes's early-period strip contribution came from Untamed and isn't there anymore.
- 🧰 **Extremely versatile.** If you need a body in a non-standard slot, Mes has probably played it already. Good flex option for tryout nights or comp experiments.

data.title: Mes.1906 — Early vs Recent Period Metrics
